note_data.title: Electromagnetic induction
keyPoints: **Electromagnetic induction** occurs when a conductor moves through a magnetic field or when a magnetic field changes near a conductor
An **induced EMF** is generated when there is relative motion between a conductor and magnetic field lines
The direction of induced current opposes the change causing it (**Lenz's law**)
**Generators** convert kinetic energy to electrical energy using electromagnetic induction
In **step-up transformers**, secondary voltage is greater than primary voltage (more turns on secondary coil)
In **step-down transformers**, secondary voltage is less than primary voltage (fewer turns on secondary coil)
**Power transmission** at high voltage reduces energy losses in cables
Transformers only work with **alternating current** because changing magnetic field is required
The **transformer equation** relates voltage to number of turns: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np

definitions.definition: The process of generating an EMF (and current if circuit is complete) when a conductor moves through a magnetic field or when magnetic field changes near a conductor
A device that converts kinetic energy into electrical energy using electromagnetic induction
A device that changes the voltage of an alternating current using electromagnetic induction
A transformer that increases voltage (secondary voltage greater than primary voltage)
A transformer that decreases voltage (secondary voltage less than primary voltage)

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give a sequence of events or detailed account of the process

modelAnswer.question: Explain why transformers are used in the electrical power transmission system from Tarbela Dam to Karachi. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Step-up transformers increase voltage for long-distance transmission. High voltage reduces current in transmission lines. Lower current reduces power losses (as heat) in the cables, making transmission more efficient.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Lower current reduces power losses/heat losses

commonMistakes: Never say 'transformers work with DC' — transformers only work with AC because changing magnetic field is needed
Students often confuse step-up and step-down transformers — check number of turns carefully
Never say 'electricity flows faster at high voltage' — high voltage reduces current, not speed
Don't forget Lenz's law — induced current always opposes the change that caused it
Students forget that EMF is induced even if no current flows (open circuit)
examinerTips: This topic appears almost every year in Paper 2, especially transformer calculations
Always state whether transformer is step-up or step-down before calculations
Power transmission questions often link to energy efficiency — mention reduced losses
Draw and label transformer diagrams showing primary/secondary coils and iron core
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Transformer diagram showing iron core with primary coil (fewer turns) connected to AC supply and secondary coil (more turns) connected to load, demonstrating step-up configuration
